binaries.title: ntfs.sys - Negative file size/offset accepted in NtSetInformationFile handlers (CWE-839), feature-gated fix

changed_functions.name: NtfsSetEndOfFileInfo
key_changes: Added call to Feature_2681941305__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage() before processing the user-supplied EndOfFile value loaded from Irp->AssociatedIrp.SystemBuffer
Added lower-bound check 'test rdi, rdi; jns' to reject negative (bit 63 set) EndOfFile values when the feature flag is enabled
Added error path returning STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER (0xc000000d) for negative EndOfFile
Old (unchecked) path retained when the feature flag is disabled
changed_functions.security_notes: NtfsSetEndOfFileInfo handles NtSetInformationFile with FileInformationClass FileEndOfFileInformation (20/0x14), dispatched by NtfsCommonSetInformation (IRP_MJ_SET_INFORMATION). It reads FILE_END_OF_FILE_INFORMATION.EndOfFile (a signed __int64) from Irp->AssociatedIrp.SystemBuffer. The unpatched code validates this only against a maximum with a signed comparison (cmp rdi,[rax+1E90h]; jg) and then commits it as the file size (mov [rbx+20h], rdi). A negative value passes the signed max check because it is smaller than every positive bound; there is no >= 0 check. The patch adds a feature-gated sign check that rejects negative values with STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER. This is not an FSCTL path.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
=== UNPATCHED: load and signed max-only check ===
0x1c00e8fef: mov     rcx, [rdi+18h]     ; rcx = Irp->AssociatedIrp.SystemBuffer
0x1c00e8ffb: mov     rdi, [rcx]         ; rdi = FILE_END_OF_FILE_INFORMATION.EndOfFile (signed)
0x1c00e9083: cmp     rdi, [rax+1E90h]   ; compare against maximum file size
0x1c00e908a: jg      loc_1c00ea058      ; signed 'greater' - negative EndOfFile not rejected
0x1c00e920f: mov     [rbx+20h], rdi     ; negative value committed as file size

=== PATCHED: added feature-gated sign check ===
0x1c00e8fdb: mov     rsi, [rdi+18h]
0x1c00e8fe7: mov     rdi, [rsi]
0x1c00e8fef: call    Feature_2681941305__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
0x1c00e8ffb: test    eax, eax
0x1c00e8ffd: jz      loc_1c00e9042      ; flag off -> skip (old behavior)
0x1c00e8fff: test    rdi, rdi
0x1c00e9002: jns     loc_1c00e9042      ; non-negative -> proceed
0x1c00e9037: mov     eax, 0c000000dh    ; STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER
0x1c00e903c: jmp     loc_1c00ea748      ; return

changed_functions.name: NtfsSetAllocationInfo
key_changes: Added call to Feature_2681941305__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage() before using the user-supplied AllocationSize
Added negative-value rejection: when the feature is enabled, rejects NewFileSize > MaximumFileSize || NewFileSize < 0 with STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER
Original max-only check (NewFileSize > MaximumFileSize) retained when the feature is disabled
changed_functions.security_notes: NtfsSetAllocationInfo handles NtSetInformationFile FileAllocationInformation (19/0x13). It reads FILE_ALLOCATION_INFORMATION.AllocationSize from SystemBuffer. Unpatched code checks only 'AllocationSize <= MaximumFileSize' (signed), so a negative AllocationSize passes. The patch adds the '< 0' rejection under the same feature flag as NtfsSetEndOfFileInfo. Same CWE-839 pattern. Reported as cosmetic in the raw diff, but it is a security-relevant validation addition.

changed_functions.name: NtfsSetPositionInfo
key_changes: New function in the patched build: the FileInformationClass 14 (FilePositionInformation) handler was extracted from NtfsCommonSetInformation
Adds feature-gated check rejecting a negative CurrentByteOffset with STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER
changed_functions.security_notes: In the unpatched build, case 14 was handled inline as 'FileObject->CurrentByteOffset.QuadPart = *(SystemBuffer)' with no validation. The patch extracts this into NtfsSetPositionInfo and, when Feature_2681941305 is enabled, returns STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER for a negative CurrentByteOffset. Same CWE-839 pattern.

changed_functions.name: NtfsCommonSetInformation
key_changes: Case 14 (FilePositionInformation) inline position assignment replaced with a call to the new NtfsSetPositionInfo
Diagnostic line-number constants and call targets shifted due to the added code
changed_functions.security_notes: IRP_MJ_SET_INFORMATION dispatcher. The dispatch logic is unchanged except that the case-14 body moved into NtfsSetPositionInfo (which carries the new validation). Listed here as behavioral because the security change lives in the extracted callee.

changed_functions.name: Feature_2681941305__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage / IsEnabledFallback + wil_* staging helpers
key_changes: New WIL feature-staging entry Feature_2681941305 added in the patched build (no references in the unpatched binary)
Standard wil_details feature-configuration plumbing updated accordingly
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-flag plumbing that gates the three validation additions. Not a security fix in itself; its runtime default is not determinable from the static image.

changed_functions.name: Diagnostic-only churn (NtfsSetValidDataLengthInfo, NtfsCheckScbForLinkRemoval, NtfsFileInfoExceptionFilter, NtfsFindTargetElements, NtfsGetSfioReservation, NtfsSetSfioReservation, NtfsMoveLinkToNewDir, NtfsRenameLinkInDir, NtfsProcessTreeForRename, NtfsStreamRename, NtfsUpdateDuplicateInfo, NtfsWalkDownTreeOnDisk, NtfsWriteUsnForRenameAndCommit, NtfsPrepareToShrinkFileSize, EfsWaitForServiceReady)
key_changes: Differ only in the numeric source-line identifiers passed to NtfsStatusTraceAndDebugInternal / NtfsRaiseStatusInternal, and a few relocated string/call addresses
Constants shifted uniformly because the inserted checks added source lines upstream
changed_functions.security_notes: No semantic or security change; verified by content diff.

security_findings.entry_point: NtSetInformationFile / ZwSetInformationFile, FileInformationClass FileEndOfFileInformation (20/0x14), dispatched by NtfsCommonSetInformation (IRP_MJ_SET_INFORMATION)
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Missing minimum-value check on an attacker-controlled 64-bit size/offset (numeric range comparison without a minimum check)
security_findings.description: NtfsSetEndOfFileInfo reads the caller's EndOfFile (a signed 64-bit LARGE_INTEGER) from Irp->AssociatedIrp.SystemBuffer. The unpatched code validates it only against a maximum with a signed comparison (cmp rdi,[rax+1E90h]; jg), then commits it as the file size (mov [rbx+20h], rdi). A negative value passes the signed max check (it is smaller than every positive bound) because there is no >= 0 check. The patch adds a feature-gated sign check (Feature_2681941305__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage + 'test rdi, rdi; jns') that returns STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER (0xc000000d) for negative values. The same class of fix is applied to NtfsSetAllocationInfo (class 19) and NtfsSetPositionInfo (class 14) under the same flag.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. Open an NTFS file with access sufficient for the target information class (write access for FileEndOfFileInformation/FileAllocationInformation). 2. Build the info buffer with a negative 64-bit value (e.g. 0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF or 0x8000000000000000). 3. Call NtSetInformationFile (or SetEndOfFile/SetFileInformationByHandle) with FileInformationClass 20 (EndOfFile), 19 (Allocation), or 14 (Position). METHOD_BUFFERED copies the buffer to Irp->AssociatedIrp.SystemBuffer. 4. Unpatched: the negative value passes the signed comparisons and is committed. Patched (flag enabled): returns STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER.
security_findings.primitive: Demonstrable effect: an attacker-supplied negative 64-bit end-of-file / allocation size / byte offset is accepted and committed to NTFS size/position bookkeeping (e.g. SCB allocation size at [SCB+0x20], or FileObject->CurrentByteOffset). No out-of-bounds read/write, information leak, or privilege-escalation primitive is demonstrable from the diff. A negative value reinterpreted as unsigned by downstream size arithmetic is a plausible but unproven route to file-metadata corruption or a bugcheck (DoS).
security_findings.assembly:
; UNPATCHED load + signed max-only check
0x1c00e8fef: mov     rcx, [rdi+18h]     ; rcx = Irp->AssociatedIrp.SystemBuffer
0x1c00e8ffb: mov     rdi, [rcx]         ; rdi = EndOfFile (signed)
0x1c00e9083: cmp     rdi, [rax+1E90h]   ; maximum file size
0x1c00e908a: jg      loc_1c00ea058      ; signed 'greater' - negative not rejected
0x1c00e920f: mov     [rbx+20h], rdi     ; negative committed as file size
; PATCHED added feature-gated sign check
0x1c00e8fdb: mov     rsi, [rdi+18h]
0x1c00e8fe7: mov     rdi, [rsi]
0x1c00e8fef: call    Feature_2681941305__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage
0x1c00e8ffb: test    eax, eax
0x1c00e8ffd: jz      loc_1c00e9042
0x1c00e8fff: test    rdi, rdi
0x1c00e9002: jns     loc_1c00e9042
0x1c00e9037: mov     eax, 0c000000dh    ; STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER
0x1c00e903c: jmp     loc_1c00ea748

breakpoints.why: IRP_MJ_SET_INFORMATION dispatcher. Break to confirm FileInformationClass is 14, 19, or 20 before it dispatches to the sub-handler.
Entry of the EndOfFile handler (unpatched). r8 = IRP, r9 = SCB.
'mov rdi, [rcx]' loads the user EndOfFile value (unpatched). Inspect its sign; a negative value is not rejected before it is committed at 0x1c00e920f.
'call Feature_2681941305__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage' followed by 'test rdi, rdi; jns' at 0x1c00e8fff/0x1c00e9002 - the added lower-bound check.

watch: rdi at 0x1c00e8ffb (unpatched): the user EndOfFile value; a value with bit 63 set is accepted
[rbx+0x20]: SCB allocation-size field where the value is committed (mov [rbx+20h], rdi at 0x1c00e920f)
[rax+0x1E90] at 0x1c00e9083: maximum file size used in the signed comparison
key_offsets: 0x1c00e920f: 'mov [rbx+20h], rdi' - value committed as file size
PATCHED 0x1c00e8fef..0x1c00e9002: feature gate + 'test rdi,rdi; jns' lower-bound check
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1. Open an NTFS file with write access. 2. Set FILE_END_OF_FILE_INFORMATION.EndOfFile (or FILE_ALLOCATION_INFORMATION.AllocationSize, or FILE_POSITION_INFORMATION.CurrentByteOffset) to a negative 64-bit value. 3. Call NtSetInformationFile with FileInformationClass 20, 19, or 14 (METHOD_BUFFERED). 4. Compare unpatched vs patched behavior.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Unpatched: the negative value passes the signed comparisons and is committed to the file's size/position field. Patched (feature enabled): the call returns STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER (0xc000000d) and nothing is committed.
debugging_aids.notes: Struct offsets: IRP+0x18 = AssociatedIrp.SystemBuffer (METHOD_BUFFERED). SCB (rbx): +0x20 = allocation size (commit target), +0xb0 -> +0x1E90 (7824) = maximum file size. The fix is gated behind WIL feature Feature_2681941305 (@ 0x1C0034380 in the patched build), which reads Feature_2681941305__private_featureState and falls back to wil_details_IsEnabledFallback; the feature is new to the patched build and its runtime default is not determinable statically. Preferred image base is 0x1C0000000.

summary: The patch adds a missing minimum-value (sign) check to three NtSetInformationFile handlers in NTFS: NtfsSetEndOfFileInfo (FileEndOfFileInformation, class 20), NtfsSetAllocationInfo (FileAllocationInformation, class 19), and the newly extracted NtfsSetPositionInfo (FilePositionInformation, class 14). Each reads a signed 64-bit LARGE_INTEGER (end-of-file, allocation size, or byte offset) from Irp->AssociatedIrp.SystemBuffer. The unpatched code validated only an upper bound with a signed comparison, so a negative value (bit 63 set) passed and was committed to the file's size/position bookkeeping. The fix rejects negative values with STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER, gated behind the WIL feature flag Feature_2681941305 (staged rollout; old path retained when off). This is CWE-839 (numeric range comparison without a minimum check), reached via NtSetInformationFile/IRP_MJ_SET_INFORMATION - not an FSCTL path. The demonstrable effect is acceptance of an invalid negative size/offset; no out-of-bounds, info-leak, or LPE primitive is demonstrable from the diff, so severity is assessed Low.
